title_en: The Quran: The Speech of Allah
content_en:
The central doctrinal battle of Ahmad ibn Hanbal's life was the question of whether the Quran was created or uncreated, and Usul al-Sunnah states his position with absolute clarity: the Quran is the speech of Allah (kalam Allah), and it is not created. This was the position of the Companions, the Successors, and the scholars of the early generations. The Mu'tazilites, drawing on Greek philosophical frameworks, argued that an eternal Quran would imply an eternal entity alongside Allah, which they held to be theologically unacceptable. Ahmad regarded this argument as a subtle but catastrophic error that undermined the foundations of Islamic theology.

Ahmad's reasoning was grounded in the texts of the Quran itself. Allah describes the Quran as His speech (kalam) in numerous verses, and the Companions understood this to mean that it was an attribute of the divine essence, not a created thing. To say that the Quran is created is, in Ahmad's view, to say that the speech through which Allah communicates with His creation is something He brought into being, which raises the further question of what existed before it and what the divine speech was before the Quran came to be. The logic of the Mu'tazilite position, he argued, leads to consequences that are more damaging to monotheism than the position they sought to avoid.

Ahmad also addressed the distinction between the Quran as an attribute of Allah and the Quran as it is recited, written, and heard by human beings. He acknowledged that the physical letters and sounds through which the Quran is expressed come into existence in time, but he was extremely cautious about articulating this distinction in ways that might give comfort to those who wanted to qualify the fundamental principle. His caution on this point led him to avoid the formulation 'my recitation of the Quran is created,' a statement he regarded as dangerously close to the Mu'tazilite position even when uttered by those who intended it to refer only to the act of recitation rather than the Quran itself.

The Mihna, the trial imposed by the caliph al-Ma'mun and continued by his successors, demanded that scholars publicly affirm the createdness of the Quran on pain of imprisonment or worse. Ahmad's refusal to comply, maintained even under physical torture, transformed him into the central symbol of Sunni resistance to theological innovation backed by state power. Usul al-Sunnah was written in the aftermath of this experience, and its treatment of the Quran reflects both the clarity of Ahmad's convictions and the gravity of what was at stake. For Ahmad, the uncreated nature of the Quran was not a peripheral theological nicety: it was the test case for whether Muslims would allow rationalist philosophy to override the testimony of revelation and the consensus of the early generations.
content_id:
Pertarungan doktrin utama dalam kehidupan Ahmad ibn Hanbal adalah pertanyaan apakah Al-Quran diciptakan atau tidak diciptakan, dan Ushul al-Sunnah menyatakan posisinya dengan kejelasan mutlak: Al-Quran adalah kalam Allah (kalam Allah), dan ia tidak diciptakan. Ini adalah posisi para sahabat, para tabi'in, dan ulama-ulama generasi awal. Kaum Mu'tazilah, dengan mengacu pada kerangka-kerangka filosofis Yunani, berpendapat bahwa Al-Quran yang azali akan mengimplikasikan wujud azali di samping Allah, yang mereka anggap tidak dapat diterima secara teologis. Ahmad menganggap argumen ini sebagai kesalahan yang halus namun bencana yang merusak fondasi-fondasi teologi Islam.

Penalaran Ahmad didasarkan pada teks-teks Al-Quran itu sendiri. Allah menggambarkan Al-Quran sebagai kalam-Nya (kalam) dalam banyak ayat, dan para sahabat memahami ini sebagai sifat zat ilahi, bukan sesuatu yang diciptakan. Mengatakan bahwa Al-Quran diciptakan adalah, menurut pandangan Ahmad, mengatakan bahwa kalam yang melaluinya Allah berkomunikasi dengan ciptaan-Nya adalah sesuatu yang Dia adakan, yang menimbulkan pertanyaan lebih lanjut tentang apa yang ada sebelumnya dan apa kalam ilahi sebelum Al-Quran ada. Logika posisi Mu'tazilah, ia berpendapat, mengarah pada konsekuensi-konsekuensi yang lebih merusak bagi tauhid daripada posisi yang ingin mereka hindari.

Ahmad juga membahas perbedaan antara Al-Quran sebagai sifat Allah dan Al-Quran sebagaimana dibaca, ditulis, dan didengar oleh manusia. Ia mengakui bahwa huruf-huruf dan suara-suara fisik yang melaluinya Al-Quran diungkapkan muncul dalam waktu, tetapi ia sangat berhati-hati dalam mengartikulasikan perbedaan ini dengan cara yang mungkin memberikan kenyamanan bagi mereka yang ingin mengkualifikasi prinsip fundamental tersebut. Kehati-hatiannya dalam hal ini membuatnya menghindari formulasi "bacaanku atas Al-Quran adalah makhluk," sebuah pernyataan yang ia anggap sangat dekat dengan posisi Mu'tazilah bahkan ketika diucapkan oleh mereka yang bermaksud mengacu hanya pada tindakan membaca bukan Al-Quran itu sendiri.

Mihnah, ujian yang diberlakukan oleh khalifah al-Ma'mun dan dilanjutkan oleh para penerusnya, mengharuskan para ulama secara terbuka menegaskan sifat makhluk Al-Quran dengan ancaman pemenjaraan atau lebih buruk. Penolakan Ahmad untuk mematuhi, yang dipertahankan bahkan di bawah siksaan fisik, mengubahnya menjadi simbol utama perlawanan Sunni terhadap inovasi teologis yang didukung oleh kekuasaan negara. Ushul al-Sunnah ditulis setelah pengalaman ini, dan perlakuannya terhadap Al-Quran mencerminkan baik kejelasan keyakinan Ahmad maupun keseruisan apa yang dipertaruhkan. Bagi Ahmad, sifat Al-Quran yang tidak diciptakan bukanlah keistimewaan teologis yang periferal: ini adalah kasus uji apakah kaum Muslim akan membiarkan filsafat rasionalis mengabaikan kesaksian wahyu dan konsensus generasi-generasi awal.
content_ar: The المحوري doctrinal battle of أحمد ابن حنبل's الحياة was the question of whether the القرآن الكريم was created or uncreated, and الأصول al-السنة states his position with absolute clarity: the القرآن الكريم is the speech of الله (علم الكلام الله), and it is not created. This was the position of the الصحابة, the التابعين, and the العلماء of the early generations. The Mu'tazilites, drawing on Greek philosophical frameworks, argued that an eternal القرآن الكريم would imply an eternal entity alongside الله, which they held to be theologically unacceptable. أحمد regarded this argument as a subtle but catastrophic error that undermined the foundations of Islamic theology. أحمد's reasoning was grounded in the texts of the القرآن الكريم itself. الله describes the القرآن الكريم as His speech (علم الكلام) in numerous الآيات, and the الصحابة understood this to mean that it was an attribute of the divine essence, not a created thing. To say that the القرآن الكريم is created is, in أحمد's view, to say that the speech through which الله communicates with His creation is something He brought into being, which raises the further question of what existed قبل it and what the divine speech was قبل the القرآن الكريم came to be. The logic of the Mu'tazilite position, احتج, leads to consequences that are more damaging to monotheism than the position they sought to avoid. أحمد أيضاً addressed the distinction between the القرآن الكريم as an attribute of الله and the القرآن الكريم as it is recited, written, and heard by human beings. He acknowledged that the physical letters and sounds through which the القرآن الكريم is expressed come into existence in time, but he was extremely cautious about articulating this distinction in ways that might give comfort to those who wanted to qualify the الأساسي principle. His caution on this point led him to avoid the formulation 'my التلاوة of the القرآن الكريم is created,' a statement he regarded as dangerously close to the Mu'tazilite position even عندما uttered by those who intended it to refer only to the act of التلاوة rather than the القرآن الكريم itself. The Mihna, the الابتلاء imposed by the caliph al-Ma'mun and continued by his التابعين, demanded that العلماء publicly affirm the createdness of the القرآن الكريم on الألم of imprisonment or worse. أحمد's refusal to comply, maintained even under physical torture, transformed him into the المحوري symbol of Sunni resistance to theological البدعة backed by state power. الأصول al-السنة was written in the aftermath of this experience, and its treatment of the القرآن الكريم reflects both the clarity of أحمد's convictions and the gravity of what was at stake. For أحمد, the uncreated nature of the القرآن الكريم was not a peripheral theological nicety: it was the الاختبار case for whether المسلمون would allow rationalist philosophy to override the testimony of الوحي and the consensus of the early generations.
